In [1]:
from lets_plot import *
import pandas as pd
In [2]:
LetsPlot.setup_html()
In [3]:
data = pd.read_csv("https://raw.githubusercontent.com/JetBrains/lets-plot-docs/refs/heads/master/data/delhi_climate.csv")
data['date'] = pd.to_datetime(data['date'])
data.head()
Out[3]:

3 2013-01-04 8.666667 71.333333 1.233333 1017.166667

In [4]:
base_plot = (
    ggplot(data) + geom_line(aes('date', 'meantemp', color='meantemp')) 
    + scale_color_gradient2(low='#0571b0', mid='#f7f7f7', high='#ca0020', midpoint=30)
)
base_plot
Out[4]:
In [5]:
# Add Fahrenheit column
data['meantemp_f'] = (data['meantemp'] * 9/5) + 32
In [6]:
fahrenheit_plot = (
    ggplot(data) + geom_blank(aes('date', 'meantemp_f')) 
)
fahrenheit_plot + ggsize(400, 200)
Out[6]:
In [7]:
def axis_color(color):
    return theme(
        axis_line=element_line(color=color), 
        axis_ticks=element_line(color=color), 
        axis_text=element_text(color=color),
        axis_title=element_text(color=color),
    )
In [8]:
ggdeck(
    [
        base_plot + labs(y='°C', color='°C'), 
        fahrenheit_plot + labs(y='°F') 
            + axis_color('blue')
            + scale_y_continuous(position='right')
    ]
) + \
ggsize(800, 400) + \
ggtitle('Dehli Mean Temperature') + \
theme_classic() + \
theme(plot_title=element_text(hjust=0.5, size=21))
Out[8]:
In [9]:
# Add Kelvin and Rankine scales.

# Alternative way: use a 'bounds' dataframe containing only the extreme coordinates to anchor the scale limits
# and ensure perfect synchronization between axes.

x_min = data['date'].min()
x_max = data['date'].max()

c_min = data['meantemp'].min()
c_max = data['meantemp'].max()
f_min = (c_min * 9/5) + 32
f_max = (c_max * 9/5) + 32

k_min, k_max = (c_min + 273.15), (c_max + 273.15)
r_min, r_max = (f_min + 459.67), (f_max + 459.67)

bounds_data = dict(
    x = [x_min, x_max],
    y_kelvin = [k_min, k_max],
    y_rankine = [r_min, r_max]
)

kelvin_plot = (
    ggplot(bounds_data) + geom_blank(aes('x', 'y_kelvin')) 
)
rankine_plot = (
    ggplot(bounds_data) + geom_blank(aes('x', 'y_rankine')) 
)
In [10]:
ggdeck(
    [
        base_plot + labs(y='°C',  color='°C'), 
        fahrenheit_plot + labs(y='°F') 
            + axis_color('blue')
            + scale_y_continuous(position='right'),
        kelvin_plot + labs(y='K')
            + axis_color('green')
            + scale_y_continuous(position='right')
            + theme(panel_inset=[0, 70, 0, 0]),
        rankine_plot + labs(y='°R')
            + axis_color('red')
            + scale_y_continuous(position='right')
            + theme(panel_inset=[0, 140, 0, 0]),
    ]
) + \
ggsize(800, 400) + \
ggtitle('Dehli Mean Temperature') + \
theme_classic() + \
theme(plot_title=element_text(hjust=0.5, size=21)) + ggtb()
Out[10]:
In [11]:
# Improved plot

ggdeck(
    [
        base_plot + labs(y='°C', color='°C'), 

        fahrenheit_plot 
            + labs(subtitle='°F') 
            + axis_color('blue')
            + scale_y_continuous(position='right')
            + theme(
                axis_title_y='blank',
                plot_subtitle=element_text(hjust=1, color='blue')
            ),
        
        kelvin_plot 
            + labs(subtitle='K')
            + axis_color('green')
            + scale_y_continuous(position='right')
            + theme(
                axis_title_y='blank',
                panel_inset=[0, 40, 0, 0],
                plot_subtitle=element_text(hjust=1, color='green')
            ),

        rankine_plot 
            + labs(subtitle='°R')
            + axis_color('red')
            + scale_y_continuous(position='right')
            + theme(
                panel_inset=[0, 80, 0, 0],
                axis_title_y='blank',
                plot_subtitle=element_text(hjust=1, color='red')
            ),
    ]
) + \
ggsize(800, 400) + \
ggtitle('Dehli Mean Temperature') + \
theme_classic() + \
theme(
    plot_title=element_text(hjust=0.5, size=21),
    panel_grid=element_line()
) + ggtb()
